commands.label: Identify Image Stack
commands.description: Shows the stacking order of the images of the current slide by placing a number over each in turn, and speaking the stack index. Image 1 is always the topmost slide in the stack of images.

commands.label: Import Selected Finder Item
commands.description:
Import the currently selected image file in the Finder into the selected image frame in Keynote.

If no image is selected in Keynote, and the slide contains a single image, it will be the recipeient of the import.

If the current slide contains multiple images, the selected image will be the recipient of the import.

If the slide has no images, the Finder item will be imported into the slide and automatically sized.

NOTE: Imported image files will include any embedded description metadata. Also, movie files are compatible for import and will be replace the contents of a target Keynote image frame.

commands.label: Import Selected Photos Item
commands.description:
Import the currently selected photo in Photos into the selected image in Keynote.

If no image is selected in Keynote, and the slide contains a single image, it will be the recipeient of the import.

If the current slide contains multiple images, the selected image will be the recipient of the import.

If the slide has no images, the exported Photos photo will be imported into the slide and automatically sized.

NOTE: imported images are automatically “linkable” back to Photos, and will include any description from Photos.

commands.label: Read the Next Slide
commands.description: This Dictation Command will perform a slide advance, and if the next slide is displayed, it will then speak the new slide’s title, body, and image descriptions. Missing items are ignored. (see the “Read this Slide” command for more deatils)

commands.label: Read the Slide
commands.description:
This Dictation Command will speak the slide’s title, body, and image descriptions. Missing items are ignored. Also, the contents of text items will be read if no title or body is showing.

NOTE: specific sounds are played before each reading indicating the type of element whose contents are about to be read. The higher sound (F/C3) indicates an image description is about to be read; the middle sound (D/C3) indicates the title is about to be read; and the lower sound (G/C2) indicates that the body is about to be read. If the contents of text items are read (see above), a secondary middle tone (C/C3) will be sounded.

commands.label: Add Orientation Keywords
commands.description: Append the appropriate oreintation keyword to the media item: landscape, portrait, square, and panorama.

commands.label: Add Rating Keyword ★
commands.description: This Dictation Command will apply a rating keyword of "★" to the selected media items. Any existing rating keyword will be removed.

commands.label: Add Rating Keyword ★★
commands.description: This Dictation Command will apply a rating keyword of "★★" to the selected media items. Any existing rating keyword will be removed.

commands.label: Add Rating Keyword ★★★
commands.description: This Dictation Command will apply a rating keyword of "★★★" to the selected media items. Any existing rating keyword will be removed.

commands.label: Add Rating Keyword ★★★★
commands.description: This Dictation Command will apply a rating keyword of "★★★★" to the selected media items. Any existing rating keyword will be removed.

commands.label: Add Rating Keyword ★★★★★
commands.description: This Dictation Command will apply a rating keyword of "★★★★★" to the selected media items. Any existing rating keyword will be removed.

commands.label: Add to Favorites
commands.description: Add the selected media items to the Favorites album

commands.label: Append Orientation Keywords
commands.description: This command will append either “landscape” “portrait” or “square” to the keywords of the selected media items.

commands.label: Assist With Adding Descriptions
commands.description: In turn, each of the selected photos will be shown full-width and a dialog will prompt you to enter the description for the currently dispalyed image.

commands.label: Assist With Adding Titles
commands.description: In turn, each of the selected photos will be shown full-width and a dialog will prompt you to enter the title for the currently dispalyed image.

commands.label: Change Export Copyright String
commands.description: This command will display a text input dialog in which you enter the copyright string to use to overlay exported images. Once a string has been stored, it will be used without prompting until this command is given t change it.

commands.label: Clear All Keywords
commands.description: After user approval, this command will clear all keywords from the selected media items.

commands.label: Clear All Titles and Descriptions
commands.description: After user approval, this command will clear all titles and descriptions from the selected media items.

commands.label: Crop Selected Photo
commands.description: Enter the crop edit mode for the currently selected photo.

commands.label: Describe with Choice
commands.description: In turn, a description of each selected item will be spoken. After each description, a dialog (shown for 5 seconds) will provide the option to Stop, Select, or Continue. If Select is chosen, the process will halt and the described item will be displayed full-screen in Photos.

commands.label: Dictated Search
commands.description: This command will search Photos for the terms or phrase dictated into a presented dialog.

commands.label: Edit Selected Photo
commands.description: Edit the selected media item.

commands.label: Exit Edit Mode
commands.description: Leave edit mode for the current image and return to albums or photos view.

commands.label: Export to File as Sequence
commands.description: Export the selected media items to files named sequentially using the user-provided base name. The media items are exported in high-quality JPEG format.

commands.label: Export to File with Default Names
commands.description: Export the selected media items to files named using the existing file names. The media items are exported in high-quality JPEG format.

commands.label: Export to New Presentation
commands.description: Export the selected media items to a new Keynote presentation.

commands.label: Export with Copyright Overlay in Black
siri_phrases_all: export this with copyright in black
commands.description: Export the selected media items to a new JPEG files overlaid with your default copyright export string. The color of the text overlay is black.

commands.label: Export with Copyright Overlay in White
commands.description: Export the selected media items to a new JPEG files overlaid with your default copyright export string. The default color of the text overlay is white.

commands.label: Favorites Slideshow
commands.description: Begin a slideshow of the contents of the Favorites album

commands.label: Install Ratings Keywords

commands.label: Last Import Slideshow

commands.label: Next Slide

commands.label: Pause Slideshow

commands.label: Previous Slide

commands.label: Remove Rating Keyword

commands.label: Remove from Favorites

commands.label: Resume Slideshow
